IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript:
This work is taking place on Buckshorn Lane
in Eye. A road closure will be in place to allow
the work to be carried out safely. Access to
your property will always be provided.
To provide a reliable electricity supply to your property, we carry out regular inspections of our
equipment in your area. This has identified a pole that needs replacing and upgrading. We will also
be installing some equipment on the new pole to ensure the cables are at statutory height. Doing
this will reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the future.To complete the work safely, we need to turn your power off to upgrade the pole and install the
new equipment. To ensure the power is off for the shortest possible time, our engineers may carry
out preparation works prior to the power cut. We will need access to the inside of your property to
conduct safety checks in the morning before we switch off the power and we will need to do
another check when we turn the power back on.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: More homes and businesses adopt low-carbon technologies such as electric vehicles heat pumps and renewable generation, energy demand on the local network is changing. We are changing the voltage settings at the transformer to help provide additional capacity for low carbon technologies, improve efficiency and reduce energy losses and help support a cleaner and more sustainable energy network. This will help the needs for net zero now and in the future. Whilst carrying out this work we will also be trimming trees that are close to the overhead cables, completing this work alongside to reduce there being further planned power cuts. Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so we can safely cut the trees away from the cables and change the voltage setting at the transformer. Doing this will reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the future as we appreciate your area has been impacted by unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months.This work is taking place on West End Goudhurst Road.

Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so a tree surgeon can safely cut the trees away from 3 sections of cables. Doing this will reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the future as we appreciate some customers may have experienced unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. This work is taking place near New Road. Multi way traffic lights will be in place on the junction of New Road, Rectory Road and Scrub Lane to allow the works to be carried out safely. Access to your property will always be provided.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Luke at 11:57 - We can now confirm that all power in the area should be back on. Our engineers had to unexpectedly turn off the power to safely repair part of our underground network in the Clifford Way area.

We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Luke at 10:58 - Thanks for your patience over today.

Our engineers have let us know that additional repairs are needed as there maybe a second fault. To give them time to do this safely, the estimated time frame to restore your power is now between 14:30 and 15:30.

We're sorry this is taking longer than expected, and we'll continue to keep you up to date, but if any change to this time comes in from our staff I'll let you know as soon as we hear.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Luke at 10:22 - We're very sorry we've had to switch off your electricity. Our engineers have turned off the power in the Clifford Way area for safety so they can make essential repairs to our underground network. Our estimated time to have your power back on is between 10:30 and 11:30, however, this may need to change later depending on how our works progress. If you'd like more information about why we sometimes have to switch power off to work; here's a short video https://UKPN.short.gy/Isol. We'll keep you updated with progress.

Steps.Message: Hi, I'm Ebony and I'm the manager of the text messaging team at UK Power Networks. Today's power cut was an unexpected underground cabling fault that affected 3984 properties, so I'm sorry that we weren't able to provide you with prior notice. I'm pleased to report our staff have resolved this by diverting power to get your lights back on as soon as possible. Were your text messages delayed during this fault? Find out why here https://UKPN.short.gy/no-sms I personally apologise for the disruption this caused you. We aim to provide a 10/10 service in keeping you updated, so if you'd like to talk to us about anything or are still without power, please call us on 105.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Great news - Our engineers have now restored power to all 3984 customers in the Billericay CM12 0 area by diverting power around an underground cabling fault.

Thank you for your patience while our team worked hard to resolve this issue safely; we're pleased that our engineers were able restore the power within the timeframe we had estimated.

If your power isn't back on, we'll need to hear from you. To report this, or to talk to us about anything else, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Simon at 15:59 - Thanks for bearing with us today.

Our engineers are checking our substation equipment to ensure our network is healthy before they attempt to restore your power. They may also walk our cabling route throughout the area to check for any visible problems, check smaller electrical sites in the area, or apply specialist equipment which may restore power temporarily to help them with their testing.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Bev at 17:00 - We can now confirm that all power in the area should be back on. Our engineers had to unexpectedly turn off the power to safely repair an underground network fault in the Fullers Close area.

We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Bev at 15:51 - We're very sorry we've had to switch off your electricity. Our engineers have turned off the power in the Fullers Close area for safety so they can repair a fault on our underground cabling network. Our estimated time to have your power back on is between 17:30 and 18:30, however, this may need to change later depending on how our works progress. If you'd like more information about why we sometimes have to switch power off to work; here's a short video https://UKPN.short.gy/Isol. We'll keep you updated with progress.

All of our texts are written and sent personally by our team, and we'll let you know once our staff arrive.
